name: Leader
text: Friendly warriors within 6 inches of this leader may use the leader's Leadership value when taking Leadership tests. The leader cannot give this benefit while knocked down, stunned or fleeing.

name: Burn the Witch!
text: This warrior hates spellcasters. Against a hated enemy in hand-to-hand combat, re-roll missed attacks in the first turn of that combat only; after that first round, fight normally.

name: Fear
text: If charged by a fear-causing enemy, take a Leadership test when the charge is declared and is in range. If failed, the warrior only hits on 6s that combat round. If the warrior wants to charge a fear-causing enemy, take a Leadership test first; if failed, the warrior does not charge and stays still. Models that cause Fear ignore Fear tests.

name: Stupidity
text: At the start of the model's turn, take a Leadership test on 2D6. If passed, the model acts normally. If failed, it cannot cast spells or fight in hand-to-hand combat until its next turn. If it is not in combat, roll D6: on 1-3 it moves directly forward at half speed, cannot charge and cannot shoot; on 4-6 it does nothing.

name: Large Target
text: Shooting attacks get +1 to hit this model. Enemy shooters may choose this model as a target if they can see it, even if it is not the closest enemy and even if it is in cover.

name: Frenzy
text: Must charge if any enemy is in charge range. Doubles the model's Attacks characteristic in hand-to-hand combat; the +1 Attack for a second weapon is added after doubling and is not doubled. Immune to other psychology while within charge range. Frenzy is lost for the rest of the battle if the model is knocked down or stunned.

name: Animosity
text: At the start of the Orc player's turn, roll D6 for each Orc or Goblin henchman not in hand-to-hand combat. On 1, roll again: 1 charges or shoots the nearest eligible friendly Orc/Goblin; 2-5 does nothing except defend itself; 6 moves as fast as possible toward the nearest enemy and may then still move in the Movement phase.

name: Animosity
text: At the start of the Forest Goblin player's turn, roll D6 for each affected Goblin henchman or Brave not in hand-to-hand combat. On 1, resolve the standard Orc and Goblin Animosity chart: a friendly fight/shot, a wasted turn arguing, or a rush toward the nearest enemy.

name: Immune to Psychology
text: The model ignores psychology effects such as Fear and does not leave combat because of psychology. This does not exempt the warband from taking Rout tests unless a specific rule says otherwise.

name: No Pain
text: Treat Stunned injury results against this model as Knocked Down instead.

name: Fanatical
text: Automatically passes Leadership-based tests. This warrior may never become the warband leader.

name: Animals
text: This fighter does not gain experience and cannot use weapons or armour unless its warband entry specifically says otherwise.

name: Prayers of Sigmar
text: Used by Witch Hunter Warrior-Priests and Sigmarite Matriarchs. Prayers are not spells, so spell-protection rules do not stop them. These models may wear armour and still use prayers. The model starts with one randomly determined prayer and may learn more instead of a new skill when allowed.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: The Hammer of Sigmar
text: Difficulty 7. The caster gains +2 Strength in hand-to-hand combat and all hits he inflicts cause double damage, such as 2 wounds instead of 1. Test each Shooting phase if he wants to keep using the Hammer.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Hearts of Steel
text: Difficulty 8. Allied warriors within 8 inches become immune to Fear and All Alone tests. The whole warband gains +1 to Rout tests. Lasts until the caster is knocked down, stunned or out of action. Multiple castings do not stack.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Soulfire
text: Difficulty 9. All enemy models within 4 inches suffer one Strength 3 hit with no armour saves. Undead and Possessed models in range suffer Strength 5 hits instead.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Shield of Faith
text: Difficulty 6. The caster is immune to all spells. At the beginning of each recovery phase, roll D6; on 1-2 the shield disappears.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Healing Hand
text: Difficulty 5. Heal one model within 2 inches of the caster, including the caster, back to full Wounds. Friendly models within 2 inches that are stunned or knocked down immediately stand up and continue fighting normally.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Armour of Righteousness
text: Difficulty 9. The caster has a 2+ armour save that replaces his normal armour save. He causes Fear and is immune to Fear. Lasts until the beginning of the caster's next Shooting phase.
source: The New Mordheimer: Prayers of Sigmar

name: Daemonic Vigour
text: Difficulty 8. Plague Bearers and Nurglings within 8 inches of the Carnival Master improve their Daemonic Aura save from 5+ to 4+ until the beginning of their next turn.

name: Buboes
text: Difficulty 7. Range 8 inches. Choose one enemy warrior in range; it must pass a Toughness test or lose 1 Wound. No armour saves are allowed.

name: Stench of Nurgle
text: Difficulty 8. Range 6 inches. All living creatures in range, friend or foe, must pass a Toughness test or lose 1 Attack until their next turn.

name: Pestilence
text: Difficulty 10. All enemy models within 12 inches of the Carnival Master suffer one Strength 3 hit. No armour saves are allowed.

name: Scabrous Hide
text: Difficulty 8. The Carnival Master gets a 2+ armour save that replaces any normal armour save. It lasts until the beginning of his next Shooting phase.

name: Nurgle's Rot
text: Difficulty 9. All enemy models in base contact with the Carnival Master immediately test against Toughness. A failed test means the model contracts Nurgle's Rot.

name: Blessings of Nurgle
text: Tainted Ones may buy Blessings only when recruited. They must start with at least one Blessing. The first Blessing uses its shown cost; each second and later Blessing for the same model costs double.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Nurgle's Rot
text: The bearer is immune to poisons. If the bearer makes a successful close-combat wound roll of 6, the living target contracts the Rot; Undead, Daemons and Possessed are immune. Before each future battle the infected warrior takes a Toughness test: fail means permanent -1 Toughness, reaching 0 Toughness kills the model, and a test roll of 6 spreads the Rot to a random warband member.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Cloud of Flies
text: Close-combat opponents suffer -1 to hit this model. The flies do not penalise the model that has the rule.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Stream of Corruption
text: Counts as a shooting attack with range 6 inches. Resolve at Strength 3 with no armour saves allowed.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Daemonic Aura
text: The model has a 5+ special armour save against wounds. The save is modified by the attack's Strength as normal and is completely negated by magic weapons and spells. The model's attacks count as magical.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Daemonic Instability
text: If taken out of action, the Daemon is destroyed on D6 roll 1-3 instead of rolling for injury. If the warband routs, each affected Daemon immediately takes a Leadership test; if failed, it counts as destroyed.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Movement
text: A Cave Squig moves 2D6 inches instead of using a fixed Movement value. It never runs or declares charges, but if this normal movement contacts an enemy it counts as charging for the next close-combat round.

name: Minderz
text: A Cave Squig must remain within 6 inches of a Goblin Warrior. If it starts its Movement phase without a Goblin within 6 inches, it goes wild for the rest of the game and moves 2D6 inches in a random direction each Movement phase, engaging any model it contacts.

name: Squig Prodder
text: A Goblin with a Squig Prodder keeps Cave Squigs within 12 inches from going wild instead of the normal 6 inches. In hand-to-hand combat, a Squig Prodder is treated exactly like a spear.

name: Warpfire
text: Difficulty 8. Range 8 inches. Hits the first model in its path for D3 Strength 4 hits, and each model within 2 inches of that target suffers one Strength 3 hit.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Children of the Horned Rat
text: Difficulty Auto. Use before the game, once per battle. Summon D3 Giant Rats within 6 inches of the Sorcerer. They disappear after the battle and do not count toward the Skaven warband size limit.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Gnawdoom
text: Difficulty 7. Range 8 inches. One model within range suffers 2D6 Strength 1 hits.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Black Fury
text: Difficulty 8. The Sorcerer may immediately charge any enemy model within 12 inches, ignoring terrain and interposing models. During that turn's hand-to-hand combat phase only, he gains +2 Attacks and +1 Strength.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Eye of the Warp
text: Difficulty 8. All standing models in base contact with the Sorcerer immediately take a Leadership test. Each failure suffers one Strength 3 hit and runs 2D6 inches directly away from the Sorcerer.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Sorcerer's Curse
text: Difficulty 6. Range 12 inches. Choose one model in range. The target must re-roll successful armour saves and successful to-hit rolls during the Skaven hand-to-hand phase and its own next shooting and hand-to-hand combat phases.
source: The New Mordheimer: Magic of the Horned Rat

name: Strike to Injure
text: Add +1 to all injury rolls caused by this warrior in hand-to-hand combat.

name: Combat Master
text: If this warrior is fighting more than one enemy model at the same time, he gains +1 Attack in each hand-to-hand combat phase while fighting two or more enemies. He is also immune to All Alone tests.

name: Weapons Training
text: This warrior may use any hand-to-hand combat weapon he finds, not only the weapons on his warband equipment list.

name: Quick Shot
text: This warrior may shoot twice per turn with a bow or crossbow. This does not apply to crossbow pistols.

name: Pistolier
text: If this warrior has a brace of pistols of any type, including crossbow pistols, he may fire twice in the Shooting phase; normal reloading rules still apply. If he has a single pistol, he may fire it in the same turn it was reloaded.

name: Weapons Expert
text: This warrior may use any missile weapon he finds, not only the missile weapons on his warband equipment list.

name: Battle Tongue
text: Leader only. Increase this warrior's Leader ability range by 6 inches. Undead leaders may not use this skill.

name: Sorcery
text: Spellcasting Heroes only. Add +1 to this warrior's rolls to cast spells successfully. Sisters of Sigmar and Warrior-Priests may not use this skill.

name: Wyrdstone Hunter
text: If this Hero searches the ruins in the exploration phase, you may re-roll one die on the Exploration chart. The second result stands.

name: Mighty Blow
text: This warrior has +1 Strength in close combat. The bonus applies to close combat weapons but does not apply to pistols.

name: Resilient
text: Deduct 1 Strength from all hits against this warrior in close combat. This does not change armour save modifiers.

name: Strongman
text: This warrior may use a double-handed weapon without the usual always-strikes-last penalty. Work out attack order normally.

name: Step Aside
text: Each time this warrior suffers a wound in close combat, he may make an extra unmodified 5+ saving throw after all armour saves.

name: Sprint
text: This warrior may triple his Movement when he runs or charges, instead of doubling it.

name: Dagger
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. The first dagger is free; extra daggers cost 2 gc. A warrior wounded by a dagger gets +1 to his armour save, or a 6+ save if he normally has no armour save.

name: Mace
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Concussion: when rolling injury for a wound caused by a mace, club or hammer, rolls of 2-4 count as Stunned.

name: Hammer
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Concussion: when rolling injury for a wound caused by a mace, club or hammer, rolls of 2-4 count as Stunned.

name: Club
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Concussion: when rolling injury for a wound caused by a mace, club or hammer, rolls of 2-4 count as Stunned.

name: Axe
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Cutting Edge: attacks with an axe have an extra -1 armour save modifier, in addition to the modifier from the user's Strength.

name: Sword
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Parry: when an opponent scores hits in hand-to-hand combat, roll a D6. If the result is higher than the opponent's highest to-hit roll, discard one hit. A model cannot parry attacks made at double or more of its own Strength.

name: Double-handed Weapon
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user +2. Two-handed: no shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat, though a shield still gives +1 save against shooting. Strike Last: the wielder always strikes last, even when charging, unless another rule such as Strongman changes this.

name: Flail
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user +2 in the first turn of each hand-to-hand combat only. Two-handed: no shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat, though a shield still gives +1 save against shooting.

name: Flail
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user +2 in the first turn of each hand-to-hand combat only. Two-handed: no shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat, though a shield still gives +1 save against shooting.

name: Halberd
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user +1. Two-handed: no shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat, though a shield still gives +1 save against shooting.

name: Spear
text: Close combat weapon. Strength as user. Strike First in the first turn of hand-to-hand combat. Unwieldy: may only use a shield or buckler in the other hand, not a second weapon. Mounted warriors using mounted rules get +1 Strength when charging with a spear for that turn.

name: Bow
text: Missile weapon. Range 24 inches. Strength 3.

name: Bow
text: Missile weapon. Range 24 inches. Strength 3.

name: Short Bow
text: Missile weapon. Range 16 inches. Strength 3.

name: Short Bow
text: Missile weapon. Range 16 inches. Strength 3.

name: Long Bow
text: Missile weapon. Range 30 inches. Strength 3.

name: Crossbow
text: Missile weapon. Range 30 inches. Strength 4. Move or Fire: the shooter may not move and fire a crossbow in the same turn, except to stand up or pivot on the spot.

name: Sling
text: Missile weapon. Range 18 inches. Strength 3. If the slinger did not move, he may fire twice in the Shooting phase, but only at targets within half range (9 inches); each shot is at -1 to hit.

name: Throwing Stars
text: Thrown missile weapon. Range 6 inches. Strength as user. No range or moving penalties apply. Cannot be used in close combat.

name: Blowpipe
text: Missile weapon. Range 8 inches. Strength 1. Poison: a natural 6 to hit automatically wounds; blowpipes cannot cause critical hits. Targets get +1 to their armour save, or a 6+ save if they normally have none. Stealthy: a hidden shooter does not automatically reveal itself; the target may pass an Initiative test to spot it.

name: Pistol
text: Blackpowder missile weapon. Range 6 inches. Strength 4. Prepare Shot: takes a full turn to reload, so it normally fires every other turn; a brace can fire every turn. Save Modifier: wounds impose -2 armour save. Hand-to-hand: with another close combat weapon, grants +1 Attack at Strength 4 with -2 save modifier, usable once per combat.

name: Brace of Pistols
text: A brace is two pistols. Range 6 inches. Strength 4. A brace can fire every turn because one pistol can be reloaded while the other fires. In the first turn of close combat, a brace gives 2 pistol attacks at Strength 4 with -2 armour save modifier; these use Weapon Skill and can be parried.

name: Warplock Pistol
text: Skaven blackpowder missile weapon. Range 8 inches. Strength 5. Prepare Shot: normally fires every other turn; a brace can fire every turn. Save Modifier: wounds impose -3 armour save. Hand-to-hand: with another close combat weapon, grants +1 Attack at Strength 5 with -3 save modifier, usable once per combat.

name: Brace of Warplock Pistols
text: A brace is two warplock pistols. Range 8 inches. Strength 5. A brace can fire every turn because one pistol can be reloaded while the other fires. In the first turn of close combat, a brace gives 2 pistol attacks at Strength 5 with -3 armour save modifier; these use Weapon Skill and can be parried.

name: Crossbow Pistol
text: Missile weapon. Range 10 inches. Strength 4. In the first round of hand-to-hand combat, the wielder may shoot before blows are struck at -2 to hit, using Ballistic Skill. This shot is in addition to close combat attacks. It can also be used as a pistol-style hand-to-hand attack once per combat at Strength 4.

name: Duelling Pistol
text: Blackpowder pistol. Range 10 inches. Strength 4. Save modifier -2. Accuracy: +1 to hit when shooting. Prepare Shot: normally fires every other turn unless using a brace. In the first round of close combat, it can be used as a pistol attack at Strength 4 with -2 armour save modifier.

name: Brace of Duelling Pistols
text: A brace is two duelling pistols. Range 10 inches. Strength 4. Save modifier -2. Accuracy: +1 to hit when shooting. A brace can fire every turn by alternating pistols. In the first round of close combat, it gives two pistol attacks at Strength 4 with -2 armour save modifier.

name: Blunderbuss
text: Blackpowder weapon. Range uses a 16 inch long by 1 inch wide fire zone. Strength 3. Every model in the fire zone is hit on 4+ regardless of shooter Ballistic Skill or cover. One shot only per battle unless reloaded by a special rule.

name: Handgun
text: Blackpowder weapon. Range 24 inches. Strength 4. Save modifier -2. Move or Fire: may not move and shoot in the same turn except to stand up or pivot. Prepare Shot: takes a full turn to reload, so it normally fires every other turn.

name: Hunting Rifle
text: Long-ranged blackpowder weapon. Range 36 inches. Strength 4. Save modifier -2. Move or Fire and Prepare Shot apply as for a handgun. Use the rifle's targeting rule from the source when choosing a target.

name: Light Armour
text: Armour save 6+. A shield improves the save by +1, so light armour and shield gives a 5+ save before modifiers. Light armour has no movement penalty when combined with a shield.

name: Heavy Armour
text: Armour save 5+. A shield improves the save by +1, so heavy armour and shield gives a 4+ save before modifiers. A warrior wearing heavy armour and shield suffers -1 Movement.

name: Shield
text: Gives a 6+ armour save if used without armour. If the warrior already has armour, improve the armour save by +1. No movement penalty from the shield itself.

name: Buckler
text: Parry. In each round of hand-to-hand combat, when an opponent scores a hit, roll D6. If the roll is higher than the opponent's highest to-hit roll, one hit is discarded. A buckler cannot parry attacks made at double or more the user's Strength.

name: Helmet
text: Avoid Stun. When this model would be stunned, roll D6. On 4+, treat the result as knocked down instead. This special save is not modified by the attacker's Strength.

name: Sigmarite Warhammer
text: Sisters of Sigmar close combat weapon. Strength as user +1. Concussion: injury rolls of 2-4 count as Stunned. Holy Weapon: +1 to wound against Possessed and Undead; critical hits still require a natural 6 to wound before modifiers.

name: Steel Whip
text: Sisters of Sigmar close combat weapon. Strength as user. Cannot be parried by swords or bucklers. Whipcrack: +1 Attack when charging; when charged, +1 Attack against the charger that strikes first. Multiple chargers still grant only +1 total. If using two whips, only one gets Whipcrack.

name: Weeping Blades
text: Skaven paired weapons. Count as two swords and grant +1 Attack for two weapons. They may parry. Venomous: permanently coated in black lotus, so no additional poison may be applied.

name: Fighting Claws
text: Skaven paired weapons. Grant +1 Attack for fighting with two weapons. Climb: improve climbing Initiative tests. Parry: may parry and re-roll one failed parry. Cumbersome: the model may not use any other weapons in the battle.

name: Stream of Corruption
text: Blessing of Nurgle. Counts as a shooting attack with range 6 inches. Resolve at Strength 3 with no armour saves allowed.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Nurgle's Rot
text: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One is immune to all poisons. If it makes a successful close-combat wound roll of 6, the living target contracts Nurgle's Rot; Undead, Daemons and Possessed are immune.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Cloud of Flies
text: Blessing of Nurgle. Close-combat opponents suffer -1 to hit the Tainted One. The flies do not penalise the Tainted One.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Bloated Foulness
text: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One gains +1 Wound and +1 Toughness, and its Movement is reduced by -1.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Mark of Nurgle
text: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One gains +1 Wound and is immune to all poisons.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Hideous
text: Blessing of Nurgle. The Tainted One causes Fear.
source: The New Mordheimer: Carnival of Chaos

name: Sign of Sigmar
text: Against Possessed or Undead opponents, the enemy loses its first Attack against this Sister in the first round of hand-to-hand combat, to a minimum of 1 Attack.

name: Protection of Sigmar
text: When a spell affects this Sister, roll D6. On 4+, the spell is nullified and affects no models at all.

name: Utter Determination
text: Matriarch only. The warband may re-roll failed Rout tests while the Matriarch is able to lead.

name: Righteous Fury
text: This Sister hates all models in Skaven, Undead and Possessed warbands. Against a hated enemy, re-roll missed close-combat attacks in the first round of that combat only.

name: Absolute Faith
text: This Sister may re-roll failed Fear tests. She also does not test for fighting alone against several opponents.

name: Tail Fighting
text: The Skaven may use its tail to carry a dagger, knife or sword for one extra close-combat attack, or carry a shield for +1 armour save. The extra tail weapon is allowed by the app's weapon limit check.

name: Infiltration
text: Deploy this warrior after both warbands are set up. Place him out of enemy line of sight and more than 12 inches from any enemy model.

name: Infiltration
text: Deploy this warrior after both warbands are set up. Place him out of enemy line of sight and more than 12 inches from any enemy model.

name: Infiltration
text: Deploy this warrior after both warbands are set up. Place him out of enemy line of sight and more than 12 inches from any enemy model.

name: Black Hunger
text: Declare at the beginning of the Skaven hero's turn. For that turn he gains +1 Attack and +D3 inches to total movement. At the end of the turn he suffers D3 Strength 3 hits with no armour saves.

name: Art of Silent Death
text: In hand-to-hand combat the Skaven may fight unarmed without penalties and counts as using two weapons, giving +1 Attack. His unarmed attacks cause critical hits on to-wound rolls of 5-6. With Fighting Claws this stacks to +2 Attacks instead of +1.

name: 'Ard Ead
text: When this Orc would be stunned, roll D6. On 3+, treat the result as knocked down instead. If he also wears a helmet, this improves to 2+ and replaces the normal helmet rule.

name: Waaagh!
text: When this Orc charges, add D3 inches to his charge range.

name: 'Ere We Go!
text: This Orc may ignore Fear and Terror tests when declaring a charge.

name: Da Cunnin' Plan
text: Orc Boss only. The warband may re-roll failed Rout tests while the Boss has not been taken out of action.

name: Well 'Ard
text: This Orc improves any armour save he has by +1.

name: 'Eadbasher
text: When this Orc causes a knocked down result in hand-to-hand combat, treat it as stunned instead.

name: See in Shadows
text: If the warrior has enough movement to reach a hidden or unseen enemy, he may roll to charge that model instead of being limited to the normal 4 inch unseen-charge range.

name: Hide in Shadows
text: When enemies try to spot this warrior while he is Hidden, halve their Initiative before measuring spotting distance.

name: Sniper
text: If Hidden, this warrior may shoot or cast a spell and remain Hidden unless the target survives and passes the source's spotting test.

name: Master of Runes
text: Shadow Weaver only. When using Elven Runestones, add +1 to the dispel roll. The Weaver may also inscribe one ally's weapons or armour so that ally may re-roll one failed armour save or Parry roll once per battle.

name: Great Hunter
text: Skink Great Crest only. Add +1 to injury rolls caused by this warrior's shooting attacks.

name: Bellowing Battle Roar
text: Saurus Totem Warrior only. If he is not hidden, in combat, knocked down, stunned or out of action, friendly Skinks within 6 inches may use his Leadership for tests.

name: Toughened Hide
text: Saurus Totem Warrior only. Improve this warrior's Scaly Skin armour save by +1.

name: Shed Animosity
text: Forest Goblin Brave only. When this advance is taken, remove or ignore that Brave's Animosity rule from then on instead of selecting a normal skill.

name: Heroes' Serious Injuries
text: Roll D66 for each Hero taken out of action.

tables.caption: Heroes' Serious Injuries
rows: Remove the hero from the roster. Weapons and equipment carried by the dead warrior are lost.
Roll D6 times on this chart. Re-roll Dead, Captured and further Multiple Injuries.
Roll D6: 1 means the arm is amputated and the hero may only use one one-handed weapon; 2-6 means miss next game.
Roll D6: 1 means the hero may not run but may charge; 2-6 means miss next game.
-1 Ballistic Skill permanently. Retire if later blinded in the remaining eye.
Before each future battle roll D6; on 1 the hero misses that battle.
The hero escapes, but all carried weapons, armour and equipment are lost.
The hero gains Hatred. Roll to determine who or what he hates.
The enemy warband holds the hero. Resolve ransom, exchange, sale, sacrifice or faction-specific result.
The hero is immune to Fear from now on.
The hero causes Fear from now on.
Fight a Pit Fighter. Losing can cause another injury; winning grants 50 gc and +2 XP.
The hero survives and gains +1 Experience.

name: Henchmen Injuries
text: Roll D6 for each Henchman taken out of action.

tables.caption: Henchmen Injuries
rows: Remove one model from the Henchman group.
The Henchman recovers and may fight in the next battle.

name: Exploration
text: Roll 1D6 for each Hero who survived without going out of action, plus one extra die if you won and any extra dice from rules. Keep a maximum of six dice. Matching dice trigger special exploration results.

tables.caption: Exploration Roll Notes
rows: Resolve the matching doubles result from the Exploration chart.
Resolve the matching triples result from the Exploration chart.
Four or more of a kind
Resolve the matching higher Exploration result if your campaign uses it.
Apply skills, equipment and hired sword effects before finalising the kept dice.

name: Full Recovery
text: D66 41-55. The hero makes a full recovery from being knocked out or lightly wounded. No lasting campaign effect.

name: Dead
text: D66 11-15. Remove the hero from the roster. All weapons and equipment carried by the dead warrior are lost and cannot be reallocated.

name: Multiple Injuries
text: D66 16-21. Roll D6 times on the Serious Injuries chart. Re-roll Dead, Captured and further Multiple Injuries results.

name: Leg Wound
text: D66 22. The hero suffers -1 Movement permanently.

name: Arm Wound
text: D66 23. Roll D6: on 1 the arm is amputated and the hero may only use a single one-handed weapon from now on; on 2-6 the hero misses the next game.

name: Madness
text: D66 24. Roll D6: on 1-3 the hero suffers from Stupidity; on 4-6 the hero suffers from Frenzy from now on.

name: Smashed Leg
text: D66 25. Roll D6: on 1 the hero may not run any more but may still charge; on 2-6 the hero misses the next game.

name: Chest Wound
text: D66 26. The hero suffers -1 Toughness permanently.

name: Blinded In One Eye
text: D66 31. The hero suffers -1 Ballistic Skill permanently. If later blinded in the remaining good eye, he must retire.

name: Old Battle Wound
text: D66 32. At the start of each future battle, roll D6. On 1, the hero misses that battle.

name: Nervous Condition
text: D66 33. The hero suffers -1 Initiative permanently.

name: Hand Injury
text: D66 34. The hero suffers -1 Weapon Skill permanently.

name: Deep Wound
text: D66 35. The hero misses the next D3 games and may do nothing while recovering.

name: Robbed
text: D66 36. The hero escapes, but all weapons, armour and equipment he carried are lost.

name: Miss Next Game
text: Generic campaign marker. The fighter is unavailable for the next battle. Use this for Arm Wound 2-6, Smashed Leg 2-6 or other source results that explicitly say the fighter misses a game.

name: Bitter Enmity
text: D66 56. The hero recovers but gains Hatred. Roll D6 to determine whether he hates the individual who injured him, that warband's leader, the whole responsible warband, or all warbands of that type.

name: Captured
text: D66 61. The hero is held by the enemy warband. Resolve ransom, exchange, sale to slavers for D6x5 gc, or faction-specific outcomes such as Undead creating a Zombie or Possessed sacrifice. Equipment is retained by the captors if the prisoner is sold, killed or turned into a Zombie.

name: Hardened
text: D66 62-63. The hero survives and is immune to Fear from now on.

name: Horrible Scars
text: D66 64. The hero causes Fear from now on.

name: Sold To The Pits
text: D66 65. The hero fights a Pit Fighter. If he loses, roll a D66 injury result from 11-35. If he survives he returns without weapons or armour. If he wins, he gains 50 gc, +2 Experience and rejoins with equipment intact.

name: Survives Against The Odds
text: D66 66. The hero survives, rejoins the warband and gains +1 Experience.

name: Hidden
text: A hidden warrior cannot be seen, shot at or charged while hidden. He may remain hidden while behind suitable cover. If he shoots, casts a spell, moves so he can be seen, or an enemy gets within its Initiative distance in inches, he is revealed and the Hidden marker is removed. A model that ran, fled, was stunned or charged cannot hide that turn.

name: Knocked Down
text: A knocked down warrior may crawl 2 inches in the movement phase but may not fight, shoot or cast spells. In combat he cannot strike back. At the start of his next turn he may stand up; that turn he moves at half rate, may shoot and cast spells, cannot charge or run, and if engaged in combat he strikes last.

name: Stunned
text: A stunned warrior may do nothing at all. In the next recovery phase, turn the model face up and treat it as knocked down.

name: Out of Action
text: The warrior is out of the game and is removed from the tabletop. It takes no further part in this battle. After the battle, resolve whether the warrior survives and whether it suffers serious lasting injuries. Play Mode automatically carries this fighter into the After Battle serious injury step.

name: Advances
text: The app detects advance thresholds crossed by comparing pre-battle XP with final XP after the battle. It queues one advance slot for each threshold and leaves the result for manual entry.
